question.text: Contract specifications for commodity derivatives typically include all of the following EXCEPT:

answer.text: The guaranteed profit or loss for the trader
Daily price limit and delivery default penalty provisions

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Contract specs include trading unit, lot size, quality specs, premiums/discounts, DPL, delivery default penalties, tender period, etc. Guaranteed P&L is never part of contract specifications. **The Trap:** All other options are actual components of contract specifications. No exchange guarantees profit or loss on a trade. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers equity derivatives contract specifications. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always read the full contract specification before trading a new commodity — delivery logistics, quality requirements, and margin rules vary significantly between commodities.
